# ferriskey_smtp_config (Resource)

Manages a FerrisKey realm's SMTP configuration. The `password` is write-only — the API never returns it, so it is kept from configuration and cannot be detected as drifted.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "ferriskey_smtp_config" "example" {
  realm      = "acme"
  host       = "smtp.example.com"
  port       = 587
  username   = "mailer"
  password   = var.smtp_password # write-only; never returned by the API
  from_email = "no-reply@acme.example"
  from_name  = "Acme"
  encryption = "tls"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `encryption` (String) Transport encryption (e.g. `tls`, `ssl`, `none`).
- `from_email` (String) From address.
- `from_name` (String) From display name.
- `host` (String) SMTP server host.
- `password` (String, Sensitive) SMTP password (write-only).
- `port` (Number) SMTP server port.
- `realm` (String) Realm whose SMTP config is managed. Also the resource ID. Changing it forces a new resource.
- `username` (String) SMTP username.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) Resource identifier (equal to `realm`).

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# Imported by realm name. Note: password cannot be imported (write-only);
# set it in configuration after import.
terraform import ferriskey_smtp_config.example acme
```
